rulings.Case Title: CROWDSTRIKE, INC. VS. DFND SECURITY, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Under Cal. Code Of Civ. Proc. Section 1008(A).
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Wednesday, May-06-2026, LINE 1. 2-RESPONDENT DFND SECURITY, INC.'S Motion For Reconsideration Under Cal. Code Of Civ. Proc. Section 1008(A). Respondent DFND Security, Inc.'s motion for reconsideration is denied. A motion for reconsideration must, inter alia, include "new or different facts, circumstances, or law." (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 1008, subd. (a).) To the extent DFND makes new arguments on this motion, it does not offer any showing of "diligence with a satisfactory explanation for not presenting the new or different information earlier." (Even Zohar Constr. & Remodeling, Inc. v. Bellaire Townhouses, LLC (2015) 61 Cal.4th 830, 833.) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: RITA JAN GURLEY M.D VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A PUBLIC ENTITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Waiver Of Objections
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Wednesday, May-06-2026, LINE 4. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion For Relief From Waiver Of Objections. The City and County of San Francisco's motion for relief from its waiver of objections pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure, section 2031.300, is granted. The court finds that the City has shown that its failure to serve a timely response was the result of mistake, inadvertence, or excusable neglect. The court further finds that the City's responses substantially comply with the relevant statutes. Nothing in this order precludes Plaintiff from pursuing her contention that the responses are insufficient through meet and confer efforts or a discovery motion. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: LEE GORDON BLUCKER ET AL VS. MIRABELLE HWANG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: HEARING FOR THE RELEASE OF RECORDS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Wednesday, May-06-2026, LINE 9. HEARING FOR THE RELEASE OF RECORDS. The court has reviewed in camera the subpoena return by AT&T and is inclined to release the records in whole to plaintiffs. The records are probative of whether defendant was making or receiving cell phone calls at the time of the incident, and the records do not contain any contents of communications. While the records contain cell phone numbers that were in contact with defendant's cell phone number, no names are included, mitigating privacy concerns. If no party contests this tentative, the records will be available with the clerk of this department for pickup by plaintiffs. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: NOURA TULIMAL VS. ZIHUI TONG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Wednesday, May-06-2026, LINE 12. DEFENDANT ZIHUI TONG's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Defendant Tong's motion to strike the claim for punitive damages is DENIED. The court liberally construes the complaint per Code of Civil Procedure section 452. Plaintiff pleads sufficient facts showing malice, oppression, or fraud to support the claim for punitive damages. (Civil Code, SEC. 3294, subd. (a).) "[M]alice does not require actual intent to harm. [Citation.] Conscious disregard for the safety of another may be sufficient where the defendant is aware of the probable dangerous consequences of his or her conduct and he or she willfully fails to avoid such consequences. [Citation]." (Pfeifer v. John Crane, Inc. (2013) 220 Cal.App.4th 1270, 1299.) Here, plaintiff's ultimate facts sufficiently allege defendant's despicable and illegal conduct. (Complaint, Ex. A, p. 5.) In Dawes v. Superior Court (1980) 111 Cal.App.3d 82, the court found the punitive damages allegations sufficient because the intoxicated driver was speeding over the posted speed limit and driving erratically. Here, plaintiff similarly pleads facts showing despicable conduct. Not only was defendant intoxicated and deliberately chose to drive, he also knowingly accepted plaintiff as a passenger while acting as a commercial driver. The court further notes that defendant failed to comply with Code of Civil Procedure, SEC. 435.5, subdivision (a). Defendant did not meet and confer with plaintiff "in person, by telephone, or by video conference" prior to filing this motion. (Id.; Knaack Decl., par. 4). A bare letter is insufficient. The court exercises its discretion to reach the merits of the motion. The court also exercises its discretion to accept and consider the late-served opposition brief. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)
